project.name: portofolio
project.domain: Personal portfolio for a designer-minded creative developer
project.runtimeConstraint: Existing Vue 3, Vite, Tailwind CSS, Lenis, GSAP, lucide-vue-next, and local fontsource packages.
project.architectureConstraint: Single-page portfolio experience using existing local data modules as content evidence.

confirmedAssumptions: The user has pivoted from the previous dark cinematic pass to a bright editorial magazine direction.
The user wants vertical scrolling rather than a full horizontal page model.
The loader must be a real entry gate that waits for content, fonts, and initial render readiness before allowing interaction.
Each section should use a different movement grammar while staying inside one editorial print system.

visualDirection.name: Editorial Magazine Issue
visualDirection.summary: A bright interactive magazine issue where each scroll is a new editorial page and every section uses a distinct print-production motion.
visualDirection.firstViewportBet: A cropped 01 chapter number, asymmetric cover spread, red line-strike reveal, and a local GitHub portrait displayed as a profile-scale square proof.
distinctiveMoves: Pre-Press Check loader blocks entry until the issue is composed.
Cover spread uses word-stagger typesetting, a line-strike reveal, and the local GitHub portrait as a right-page square profile proof.
Editor note uses column reflow motion.
Process grid uses typesetting rule lines drawn before copy appears.
Project spreads alternate compositions instead of using repeated cards.
Career reads as an issue index and contact reads as a back cover.

motionPaletteDecision.decision: Use bright editorial print motion with paper white, black ink, vivid red, active blue, and yellow proof marks.
motionPaletteDecision.densityDecisionSource: The user explicitly wants a designer/agency-level personal portfolio and asked for unique section motion.
motionPaletteDecision.spatialDecision: Use vertical editorial pages and GSAP ScrollTrigger rather than a horizontal-only model. No WebGL is needed for this pass because print-production motion delivers the selected anchor.

conceptualAnchor.name: Indonesian editorial magazine pre-press issue
conceptualAnchor.anchorReference: Premium printed magazine production: pre-press marks, asymmetric spreads, cropped chapter numbers, column rules, issue index, and back cover
conceptualAnchor.specificReferencePoint: A magazine issue being checked before print, then opened through cover, editor note, feature spreads, index, and back cover.
conceptualAnchor.rationale: The anchor gives a coherent reason for the loader, section layout, palette, typography, and motion variety without copying any individual portfolio.
conceptualAnchor.signatureMotion: Pre-press marks calibrate, a red strike line pulls open the issue, then each section uses a different print-production reveal.
conceptualAnchor.typographicDecision: Bebas Neue carries chapter numbers and issue markers, Fraunces carries editorial headlines and quotes, and Manrope carries body, metadata, stack tags, and controls.
conceptualAnchor.visualRiskBudget: High visual authorship with WCAG contrast, keyboard access, and reduced-motion fallback.
conceptualAnchor.motionRiskBudget: Motion can be expressive, but content must be rendered before entry and readable without animation.

derivedTokenLogic.colorDerivationSource: Paper white and warm stock for editorial surfaces, black ink for main reading, red for issue marks and line-strike motion, blue for technical links/status, yellow for proof marks.
derivedTokenLogic.spacingDerivationSource: Large margins, strong gutters, rule lines, and cropped chapter numbers follow magazine spread composition.
derivedTokenLogic.typographyDerivationSource: Display numbers, editorial serif headlines, and clean sans metadata create role contrast.
derivedTokenLogic.motionDerivationSource: Pre-press calibration, typesetting stagger, column reflow, rule-line draw, page lift, index tick, and sticker/back-cover reveal.
derivedTokenLogic.validationRule: Every motion and line must map to a print-production, chapter, navigation, or evidence function.

aiSafeUiAudit.failureDefinition: A generic portfolio would use centered hero text, equal project cards, soft shadows, and decorative grid wallpaper.
aiSafeUiAudit.interchangeabilityTest: Renaming this to a SaaS or agency site would break the issue numbers, pre-press loader, editorial section grammar, and project feature spreads.
requiredProductSpecificSignals: Issue 01 marker visible in the first viewport.
Project evidence is presented as editorial feature spreads with constraint, decision, and outcome.
The loader behaves as pre-press composition and gates actual entry until content is ready.
aiSafeUiAudit.backgroundPatternRule: Rules and lines are allowed only as column, chapter, loader, or issue markers.

paletteRoles.purpose: chapter marker, CTA, loader strike line

colorTruth.productSpecificBehavior: Red marks entry and chapter motion, blue marks technical proof, yellow marks pre-press proofing.

tokenSystem.implementationNote: CSS custom properties mirror the documented palette and Tailwind aliases.

mutationRules.mobile: Single-column issue flow, smaller chapter numbers, section motion simplified to opacity/translate, project spreads stack.
mutationRules.tablet: Two-column editorial groups appear for process/career while project spreads remain vertical.
mutationRules.desktop: Asymmetric cover spread, sticky issue rail, large cropped chapter numbers, and varied feature-spread layouts.

motionSystem.loader: Pre-Press Check overlay waits for initial render, fonts, image preload, and minimum stage duration before unlocking scroll.
sectionSignatures: cover: word stagger, red strike line, and local square portrait proof
process: rule line draw then cell reveal
projects: alternating feature-spread lift and metadata wipe
motionSystem.library: GSAP ScrollTrigger for scroll-bound section choreography, scoped with gsap.matchMedia for desktop behavior.
motionSystem.reducedMotion: Disable transforms and GSAP choreography; all content renders visible and loader exits quickly after readiness.

rules: Borders and rule lines must express magazine structure.
Repeated projects must vary layout by feature spread variant.

designExecutionPolicy.oldDesignRegressionTest: The result must not read as the previous portfolio simplified or as dark cinematic residue.

designExecutionHandoff.primaryExperienceGoal: Make the first impression feel like a personal portfolio designed at agency taste level while keeping project evidence scannable.

surfacePlan.goal: Gate entry until content is ready and introduce the issue language.
Establish designer-minded developer positioning with the local GitHub portrait as a right-page square profile proof.
State the design/product point of view.
Show working method as an editorial system.
Show real projects with varied layouts.

libraryDecisions.purpose: Word-stagger, loader timeline, and scroll section choreography.
libraryDecisions.stableVersion: package.json ^3.15.0, official docs support npm install and plugin registration.

libraryDecisions.purpose: Scroll-bound section reveals and class/state choreography.

libraryDecisions.stableVersion: Added in GSAP 3.11 and compatible with current package range.

genericitySignals: old dark cinematic palette remains dominant
portfolio behaves as a simplified old design
all sections use the same fade-up motion
loader is a fake progress animation without readiness gating
validBoldSignals: project evidence remains readable and varied

taintedSources: old cinematic implementation as final style
contextHygiene.approvedExternalConstraintUsage: Extract print-production and editorial composition constraints only.

forbiddenPatterns: loader that does not gate real readiness
